title: Arachnorchis lodgeana
intro: Arachnorchis lodgeana, also known as the Lodge's spider orchid, is a rare and endangered species of orchid native to Australia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, as well as its ability to protect against cell damage and improve immune function. The bioactive constituents of Arachnorchis lodgeana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Arachnorchis lodgeana is often prepared as a tincture, capsule, or infusion, and is used to treat a range of health issues, including stress, anxiety, and digestive problems.
